---
title: "Glim -- Handoff File"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

# Glim -- Handoff File

**Last Updated:** 2026-03-25

**Purpose:** Operational coordination between Claude instances and across sessions. Tracks what happened recently, what needs to happen next, and how to work in this project's environment. Updated every session.

**Key principle:** Reference the Project Documentation for explanations rather than restating them. Keep entries terse and action-oriented.

**Companion document:** `glim_project_documentation` (.Rmd / .docx) is the single source of truth for how Glim works, why it works that way, and full feature/decision history. This handoff file is for session-to-session coordination. If you need to understand a feature, go to the Project Documentation. If you need to know what to do next, stay here.

---

### DOCUMENT MAINTENANCE RULES:
- **ADD** new information to appropriate sections; do not remove existing content unless archiving
- **EDIT** only when specific information needs correction
- **ARCHIVE** session log entries older than 4 weeks once their content has been captured in the Project Documentation
- **REFERENCE** the Project Documentation for explanations rather than duplicating them (format: [See Project Docs Section X.X: Title])

---

# 1. Instance Instructions

Read the section that matches your instance. Each contains a complete session protocol and role-specific guidance.

---

## 1.1 Instructions for Claude.ai

### Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read the Project Documentation (if uploaded or if significant design/feature questions are expected)
- Read this Handoff File
- Check "Changes for Testing" (Section 3.2) for outputs that need browser verification
- Review recent Session Log entries (Section 2) to understand what has been done since your last session
- Check "Open Questions" (Section 4) for items that may have been resolved or need attention

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- When editing `desktop-pet.jsx` or `glim.html`, always verify structural integrity after changes (bracket balance, key functions present, storage keys intact)
- When making significant design or technical decisions, document them using the Decision Register format in Project Documentation Section 9
- When drafting tasks for Claude Code, write them as actionable entries with clear acceptance criteria for the Pending Tasks queue (Section 3.1)
- When adding or editing messages, follow the voice rules: lowercase, casual, no em dashes, personal references by name, science facts prefixed with casual tags. No hustle culture, toxic positivity, or pressure to perform.
- After code changes, always rebuild the HTML export and spot-check the output

**AT SESSION END:**

- Promote Session Log content to the Project Documentation as appropriate:
  - Feature additions/changes go in Project Documentation Sections 4-5
  - Significant decisions go in Project Documentation Section 9 (Decision Register)
  - Work summaries go in Project Documentation Section 10 (Project Log)
- Move any tested/verified changes from "Changes for Testing" (Section 3.2) to "Completed Tasks" (Section 3.3)
- Add any new tasks to "Pending Tasks" (Section 3.1)
- Flag any open questions or blockers in Section 4

### Role and Responsibilities

**Primary responsibilities:**

- Feature design and implementation (JSX/React development in artifact environment)
- Message writing and content creation (maintaining Glim's voice and personalization)
- Visual design decisions (creature appearance, animation, background, UI)
- HTML export build process (tail/sed/cat pipeline or equivalent)
- Project Documentation maintenance (features, decisions, limitations, roadmap)
- Design review and iteration with the user

**Development workflow (current):**

1. Edit `desktop-pet.jsx` in Claude artifact
2. Test changes in artifact preview
3. Run the HTML export build process
4. Download `glim.html` and verify key elements
5. User saves to same file path and refreshes browser tab

**Quality checks after code changes:**

- Bracket/paren/brace balance in the script block
- All 6 component functions present (Background, AmbientBugs, SpeechBubble, PersistentReminder, OwlMoth, DesktopPet)
- Storage keys present: `glim-journal`, `glim-pokes`, `glim-settings`
- No em dashes in any message strings
- All `showMessage` calls match current signature: `showMessage(text, wellness?, msgType?)`
- Timer effects have cleanup returns
- New refs follow the sync-to-state pattern (see Project Docs Section 2.3)

**Message content rules:**

- Lowercase, casual tone
- No em dashes (use single hyphens)
- Personal references by name (Spooky, Pepita, Alex, Lados, Anora, Dr. Blanco-Suarez)
- Science facts prefixed with casual tags ("science fact:", "nerd fact:", "did you know:", etc.)
- No hustle culture, toxic positivity, or pressure to perform
- See Project Docs Section 5 for full message inventory and themes

---

## 1.2 Instructions for Claude Code

### Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read this Handoff File
- If the task involves feature logic, message content, or design decisions, ask the user to provide the Project Documentation or relevant sections
- Check "Pending Tasks" (Section 3.1) for assigned work items
- Review the File System Map (Section 6) to orient yourself to the project structure
- Check Computational Environment (Section 7) for environment and dependency info
- Review Code and Style Preferences (Section 8) before writing any code

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- When writing build scripts, deployment configs, or tooling, use the header format and style conventions from Section 8
- When touching message content or creature behavior logic, cross-reference with the Project Documentation for design context and voice rules
- If a task references a feature or design decision you don't have context for, ask the user for the relevant Project Documentation section rather than guessing
- Pause between major steps, report what changed, and confirm before proceeding

**AT SESSION END:**

- Update Session Log (Section 2) with a dated entry of work done, including:
  - Files created or modified (with paths)
  - Output files generated (with paths)
  - Any checks performed and their outcomes
  - Any warnings, unexpected results, or concerns
- Move completed tasks to "Completed Tasks" (Section 3.3) with date and output location
- Add any changes needing browser verification to "Changes for Testing" (Section 3.2) with:
  - File paths to the outputs
  - What to look for when testing
  - Any notes or observations
- Update File System Map (Section 6) if files were created, moved, or deleted
- Update Scripts Reference table (Section 6.2) if new scripts were created
- Flag any issues or questions in Section 4

### Role and Responsibilities

**Primary responsibilities:**

- Build tooling and automation (HTML export script, build pipeline)
- GitHub repository setup and maintenance
- PWA file creation (manifest.json, service worker, app icons)
- GitHub Pages deployment configuration
- File system operations (organizing, renaming, packaging)
- Automated testing or verification scripts
- Any task that benefits from persistent file system access and shell execution

**Planned near-term tasks:**

- Create permanent HTML export build script (replace the ad-hoc tail/sed/cat pipeline)
- Set up GitHub repository with proper .gitignore
- Create PWA files (manifest.json, sw.js, icon generation from SVG)
- Configure GitHub Pages deployment
- Potentially: automated structural verification script for post-edit checks

**Check-in format after major steps:**

```
Step [X] complete.
  Results:
  - Output file: [path]
  - [Key detail]: [value]
  Checks: [what was verified]
  [Notes or concerns]
  Ready to proceed to Step [Y]. Continue? (y/n)
```

**Red flags to watch for and report:**

- HTML export missing storage polyfill or CDN links
- Bracket/brace imbalance in generated HTML
- Missing component functions after export transformation
- File size anomalies (glim.html should be ~2400-2500 lines currently)
- Service worker caching stale versions
- Git conflicts in the single-file codebase

---

# 2. Session Log

Both instances update this log. Claude.ai promotes substantive content to the Project Documentation as appropriate (features to Sections 4-5, decisions to Section 9, milestones to Section 10).

> **Entry template:**
>
> ### [DATE] ([Instance])
> - [What was done]
> - [Key outputs generated with file paths]
> - [Checks performed and outcomes]
> - [Decisions made - reference Project Docs Decision Register if significant]
> - [Issues encountered or flagged]

### 2026-03-27 (Claude Code) - Zustand migration, hosting migration, icon/splash, file separation

**Hosting migration (Netlify -> GitHub Pages)**
- Migrated from Netlify to GitHub Pages to avoid credit exhaustion from deploy previews
- Created `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`: triggers on push to main, builds with Firebase secrets injected, deploys to GitHub Pages
- Added `base: '/Glim/'` to `vite.config.js`, updated `manifest.json` start_url and icon paths to include `/Glim/` prefix
- Fixed `SignIn.jsx` icon path using `import.meta.env.BASE_URL` for GitHub Pages compatibility
- Added Firebase secrets to GitHub repository Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions (6 VITE_FIREBASE_* vars)
- Live URL: https://reitheheroine.github.io/Glim/
- Netlify project can be deleted when convenient (no active traffic)

**iPhone UI fixes**
- Safe area gap at bottom: color-matched div strip using `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` + `hsla()` matching ground color
- Settings/journal buttons: moved to `calc(env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px) + 16px)` from top
- Mountain path: reverted leftmost mountain after user rejected repositioned version

**Icon and splash screen (from Claude.ai handoff files)**
- Copied `glim-icon-multihue.svg` to `client/public/glim-icon.svg`
- Generated PNG icons using `sips`: `glim-icon-192.png`, `glim-icon-512.png`, `apple-touch-icon.png`
- Moved `SplashScreen.jsx` to `client/src/SplashScreen.jsx`
- Updated `App.jsx` to use `<SplashScreen />` while Firebase auth resolves (replaces blank dark div)

**Sign-out button**
- Added sign out button to settings panel (`signOut(auth)` on click)

**Zustand state migration**
- Installed `zustand` npm package
- Created `client/src/utils.js`: `getTimeOfDay()`, `getColors()`, `pickRandom()` (extracted to avoid circular imports)
- Created 6 domain stores in `client/src/stores/`:
  - `useCreatureStore.js`: mood, hue/sat, all animation state, drag/motion state
  - `useMessageStore.js`: speech bubble text/visibility, reminder state
  - `useSettingsStore.js`: intervals with localStorage persistence + `lastModified` for sync compatibility
  - `useUIStore.js`: panel visibility, journal view/text/prompt state
  - `useJournalStore.js`: entries with localStorage persistence, soft-delete, reload action
  - `usePokesStore.js`: poke counter with localStorage persistence
  - `index.js`: barrel export
- Migrated DesktopPet.jsx: replaced 35+ useState calls with store reads, removed local reload callbacks
- Fixed several migration regressions: duplicate declarations, removed `useState` accidentally dropped from React import (Background uses it for shootingStar)
- Build verified clean after migration

**File separation**
- Split monolithic DesktopPet.jsx (1670 lines) into 9 files:
  - `client/src/components/Background.jsx`
  - `client/src/components/AmbientBugs.jsx`
  - `client/src/components/SpeechBubble.jsx`
  - `client/src/components/OwlMoth.jsx`
  - `client/src/components/PersistentReminder.jsx`
  - `client/src/components/SettingsPanel.jsx` (reads stores directly, includes gear button)
  - `client/src/components/JournalPanel.jsx` (reads stores directly, `onSave` prop for cross-store callback)
  - `client/src/glim-animations.css` (all keyframes, imported once in DesktopPet.jsx)
  - DesktopPet.jsx retained as main logic component (~430 lines)
- `npm run build` clean; pushed and verified on GitHub Pages

### 2026-03-27 (Claude.ai) - Feature design brainstorm, icon design, and build plan
- Brainstormed and designed feature suite: nutrition (hybrid library + raw add), pomodoro/time tracking (with work categories), to-do list (recurring via Approach 2, done section), step tracking (replace-style, tier milestones), workout logging (categories, intensity, per-type goals)
- Designed home screen layout: big Glim + 3 summary widgets (steps ring, water ring, tasks done) + nav bar (home/focus/tasks/nutrition/more)
- Mockupped companion mode panels (nutrition quick-add) and home screen variations (rings-only vs all-widgets, chose rings-only)
- Designed nav bar icons: nest (home), leaf (nutrition), clipboard (tasks), clock (focus), three dots (more)
- Established 5-phase inside-out build plan: (1) scaffolding + prerequisites, (2) water tracker, (3) second companion tracker, (4) to-do list (first focus mode), (5) everything else
- Designed and finalized multi-hue app icon (accurate Glim from DesktopPet.jsx bezier paths) and splash screen with "oh hi, welcome back!" randomized taglines
- Output files for Claude Code: `glim-icon-multihue.svg`, `SplashScreen.jsx`, `icon-splash-handoff.md`
- Updated Project Documentation: Sections 8.2.1, 8.3, 8.4, 8.8, 8.10, 6 decision register entries, project log
- Updated Handoff: task queue restructured by build phase, session log, resolved questions

### 2026-03-26 (Claude.ai) - Mobile platform strategy evaluation
- Evaluated four mobile deployment options: enhanced PWA, Capacitor (WebView wrapper), React Native, full native (Swift/Kotlin)
- Analyzed native API requirements vs. PWA capabilities. Only meaningful gap: HealthKit/Google Fit (requires $99/year Apple Developer Program on iOS)
- Confirmed Web Push notifications work on iOS for home-screen PWAs (since iOS 16.4, clunky onboarding but functional)
- Confirmed React Native would require rewriting all visual components (~70% of codebase), not justified
- Decision: stay on enhanced PWA path, Capacitor as documented fallback if native API need arises [See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-26: Enhanced PWA over native app]
- Updated Project Documentation: platform strategy (Section 8.1), decision register entry, project log entry
- Updated Handoff: new Web Push notification task (Medium priority), bumped service worker task from Low to Medium, added session log
- No code changes this session

### 2026-03-26 (Claude Code) - Firebase setup and Google auth flow
- Installed `firebase` npm package (v11.x) in `client/`
- Added `.env.local` (gitignored) with all Firebase config vars as `VITE_FIREBASE_*`
- Added `.env.example` (committed, no secrets) as template for config vars
- Updated `.gitignore` to exclude `.env.local` and `.env.*.local`
- Created `client/src/firebase.js` - initializes Firebase app, exports `db` (Firestore), `auth` (Firebase Auth), `googleProvider` (GoogleAuthProvider)
- Created `firestore.rules` - restricts all reads/writes to authenticated user's own `users/{userId}/**` path; deployed to Firebase Console
- Created `client/src/SignIn.jsx` - full-screen twilight-aesthetic sign-in screen with Google sign-in button; handles popup errors and loading state
- Updated `client/src/App.jsx` - auth gate: undefined=loading (blank dark screen), null=show SignIn, user object=show DesktopPet; creates Firestore `users/{userId}` document on first sign-in (name, email, createdAt) using getDoc check so createdAt is never overwritten
- Checks: `npm run build` clean (628KB JS / 194KB gzip - Firebase adds ~100KB gzip); sign-in flow tested locally at localhost:5174; Firestore user document created on first login; pushed to Netlify and confirmed live
- Notes: Google sign-in provider must be enabled in Firebase Console > Security > Authentication > Sign-in method. JS bundle is now over Vite's 500KB warning threshold - cosmetic only, no action needed now.

### 2026-03-25 (Claude.ai)
- Full line-by-line codebase audit of `glim.html` against Project Documentation
- Fixed 6 bugs: timer memory leaks in scheduleBlink/scheduleTwitch/shootingStar, wander drift orphan cleanup, behavior system rewrite (cascading draws to weighted random), eye tracking callback stabilization
- Added settings persistence (`glim-settings` storage key)
- Removed dead code (`calcDuration`, unused `durationOverride` param)
- Corrected documentation-code mismatches: marked off-screen adventure, touch events, responsive layout, `isMobile` as [NOT IMPLEMENTED]
- Updated Project Documentation with 9 [NOT IMPLEMENTED] tags, new Section 7.1, 4 new Decision Register entries, 1 new Project Log entry
- Outputs: updated `glim.html` (~2461 lines), updated `glim_project_documentation.Rmd` and `.docx`
- Created this Handoff File

### 2026-03-25 (Claude.ai) - Architecture review and feature planning
- Evaluated React vs vanilla JS migration. Decided to stay on React given planned feature complexity (10+ data-driven trackers). See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-25.
- Discussed cross-device sync. Decided on Firebase with offline-first design. Data model to be designed now, Firebase integration built later.
- Decided on event-based data model for all trackers (timestamped logs, not running totals) for sync-safe merging.
- Decided on centralized Glim reaction system (`glimReact(event, data)`) to keep personality separate from tracker code.
- Cataloged ~25 features across 7 categories. Rewrote Project Docs Section 8 as comprehensive feature roadmap with data model notes.
- Outputs: updated `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/`.docx` (Section 1.1, Section 8 rewrite, 4 new Decision Register entries, 1 new Project Log entry), updated `glim_handoff.Rmd`/`.docx`

### 2026-03-25 (Claude Code) - Netlify hosting and PWA setup
- Set up Netlify hosting: connected to `ReitheHeroine/Glim` repo, build dir `client/`, publish dir `client/dist/`, command `npm run build`. Auto-deploys on every push to `main`.
- Added `client/public/_redirects` to fix Netlify "Page not found" routing issue
- Removed `base: '/Glim/'` from `vite.config.js` (was for GitHub Pages, Netlify serves from root)
- Created `client/public/glim-icon.svg` - custom Glim icon (dark background, purple/teal creature with eyes, wings, antennae, beak)
- Generated PNG icons from SVG using `sips`: `glim-icon-192.png`, `glim-icon-512.png`, `apple-touch-icon.png` (180px)
- Created `client/public/manifest.json` - PWA manifest (standalone display, dark theme, both icon sizes)
- Updated `client/index.html` with PWA meta tags: `apple-touch-icon`, manifest link, `apple-mobile-web-app-capable`, status bar style, theme-color
- Checks: `npm run build` clean; Netlify deploy successful; Glim confirmed working in browser
- Install path for iPhone: open Netlify URL in Safari → Share → Add to Home Screen

### 2026-03-25 (Claude Code) - Vite migration
- Initialized Vite + React project scaffold in `client/` directory (React 19, Vite 8)
- Installed Tailwind CSS v4 with `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin (already configured in vite.config.js)
- Extracted all message pools (lines 52-757 of `glim.html`) to `client/src/messages.js` with `export` on each const
- Created `client/src/storage.js` - window.storage polyfill (localStorage wrapper) as side-effect module
- Created `client/src/DesktopPet.jsx` - all components + logic (utils, Background, AmbientBugs, SpeechBubble, OwlMoth, PersistentReminder, DesktopPet) with proper React imports and messages/storage imports
- Updated `client/src/App.jsx` to render DesktopPet only
- Updated `client/src/index.css` with Tailwind v4 import and Glim base styles (overflow:hidden, 100% height)
- Updated `client/index.html` with correct title ("Glim") and owl emoji favicon
- Removed scaffold files (App.css, assets/)
- Added `.gitignore` at repo root
- Checks: `npm run build` - clean, no errors (285KB JS bundle); `npm run dev` - serves at localhost:5173, HTTP 200
- Notes: React version is 19 (not 18 as planned in docs) - no issues encountered. Zustand not yet installed (assigned to Claude.ai). GitHub repo not yet initialized (no `git init` run).

### 2026-03-25 (Claude.ai) - Tech stack evaluation
- Evaluated entire technology stack against 25-feature roadmap
- Decided: adopt Vite as build tool (eliminates Babel CDN, enables file separation, dev server)
- Decided: stay on React over Svelte (ecosystem size, skill transferability, existing code)
- Decided: adopt Zustand for state management (replaces 30+ useState calls)
- Decided: Tailwind stays but migrates from CDN to build-integrated version
- Decided: Chart.js for data visualization, vite-plugin-pwa for PWA generation
- Updated Project Docs: Section 2.1 (current + planned stack), Section 8.2 (infrastructure items), Section 11 (tools/references), 3 new Decision Register entries
- Updated Handoff: task queue (Vite migration replaces build script), computational environment, resolved questions

### 2026-03-26 (Claude.ai) - Firestore schema design and sync architecture decisions
- Reviewed three sync approaches from previous session. Confirmed Approach 2 (localStorage-primary with background Firebase sync) as the implementation path.
- Decided: Google sign-in for authentication (one-tap, most users have Google accounts).
- Decided: Require sign-in on first launch (eliminates local-to-cloud migration problem entirely). Every event written to Firestore from the start, localStorage is purely an offline cache.
- Decided: Pattern A (subcollections per domain) for Firestore top-level structure. Each tracker domain gets its own subcollection under `users/{userId}/`. Scales naturally, avoids 1MB document limit, writes are small and fast.
- Decided: Pokes stored as single counter document (not event log). Simpler, and losing a few pokes during offline conflicts is acceptable for a vanity counter.
- Designed concrete Firestore schema for current 3 domains only (journal, pokes, settings). Future tracker schemas will be added incrementally as features are built, not speculatively.
- Firestore path structure finalized: `users/{userId}/journal/{entryId}`, `users/{userId}/pokes/counters`, `users/{userId}/settings/current`
- User metadata (name, email, createdAt) stored on the `users/{userId}` document itself.
- Outputs: updated `glim_handoff.Rmd`

### 2026-03-25 (Claude.ai) - Data model, reaction system, and UI design
- Designed complete data model spec: 15 storage domains with full schemas, sync strategies, migration paths. Foundational decisions: one key per domain, UUIDs, local dates from UTC, soft delete everywhere. Documented as Project Docs Section 8.8.
- Designed reaction system: 4-tier pipeline (comfort/acknowledge/celebrate/milestone), template interpolation, context evaluation (sleep check, cooldown, dedup), multi-event sequences, nudge events. Walked through water and mood tracking examples. Documented as Project Docs Section 8.9.
- Designed UI navigation system: hybrid companion/focus modes, customizable 4-slot bottom nav bar, mini-Glim spec for focus mode. Created mockups in HTML. Documented as Project Docs Section 8.10.
- Discussed AI-powered messages (hybrid static + Claude API for summaries/cross-tracker observations). Deferred to after Firebase - added as a known future option.
- Outputs: updated `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/`.docx` (Sections 8.8-8.10 fully specified, 2 new Decision Register entries, 2 new Project Log entries), `mockup_companion_mode.html`, `mockup_focus_mode.html`

---

# 3. Task Queue

## 3.1 Pending Tasks

Tasks are organized by build phase (see Project Docs Section 8.2.1). Work within each phase roughly top-to-bottom, but exact order within a phase is flexible.

---

### PHASE 1: Minimal scaffolding (prerequisites + nav shell)

#### Implement touch event support
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (prerequisite for mobile panels)
- **Description:** Add Pointer Events API support. Create `getPointerXY()` helper that extracts coordinates from either mouse or touch events. Attach pointer handlers on all interactive elements. Add `touch-action: none` on draggable elements. Primary blocker for mobile deployment.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 3.4.2: Where Code Differs Between Platforms]
- **Acceptance criteria:** All interactions (click, double-click, long press, drag, shake) work on both mouse and touch. Eye tracking follows touch/pointer position.

#### Implement responsive layout
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (prerequisite for mobile panels)
- **Description:** Add `isMobile` flag based on `window.innerWidth < 600` with resize listener. Scale creature size, glow ring, panel widths, and button sizes. Ensure tap targets meet 44px minimum on mobile. Settings and journal panels expand to near-full-width on small screens.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 3.4.5: Implementation Status]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Glim renders correctly on screens from 320px to 1920px+. Panels don't overflow on small screens. Tap targets are 44px+ on mobile.

#### Build nav bar shell
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (enables feature panels)
- **Description:** Implement the persistent bottom nav bar with 5 slots: home (fixed, nest icon), focus (clock), tasks (clipboard), nutrition (leaf), more (three dots). Home returns to companion mode. Other slots are placeholders for now (show "coming soon" or do nothing). Active slot highlighted in teal. Nav bar appears in both companion and focus modes.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.10: Bottom navigation bar]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Nav bar renders on both desktop and mobile. Home icon returns to creature view. Active state visually indicated. Bar is persistent across mode transitions.

#### Build companion mode panel mechanism
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (enables tracker panels)
- **Description:** Build the slide-up panel system for companion mode. Panel slides up from bottom over 300ms, drag handle to dismiss, tap-outside to dismiss, creature compresses to ~90% when panel open, smooth transition between panels. Only one panel open at a time. Panel height ~40% of screen.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.10: Companion mode details]
- **Acceptance criteria:** A test panel can be opened and dismissed via nav bar tap, drag-down, and tap-outside. Creature compresses and expands smoothly. Works on both mouse and touch.

---

### PHASE 2: Water tracker (proves the full pattern)

#### Build Glim reaction system
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (prerequisite for water tracker and all subsequent trackers)
- **Description:** Implement the centralized `glimReact(eventType, data)` system. Define event types for existing features (journal save, wellness reminder dismissed) and water tracker (water logged, goal met, streak). Refactor existing journal save reaction to use the system as proof of concept.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.9: Reaction System Specification]
- **Acceptance criteria:** `glimReact()` function exists and handles message selection, animation triggering, and personality context. Existing journal save uses it. Water tracker can call it without knowing how creature responses work.

#### Build water tracking
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (pattern-prover for all trackers)
- **Description:** First tracker feature. Companion mode panel with progress ring, tap-to-log, undo toast. Zustand store + localStorage persistence. Integrates with `glimReact()`. Progress ring widget on home screen. Proves the full data flow: UI -> Zustand -> localStorage -> glimReact() -> creature response -> home screen widget update.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.3: Daily Tracking], [See Project Docs Section 8.8: Water schema]
- **Acceptance criteria:** User can log water, see progress ring fill, and see Glim react. Data persists across refresh. Home screen widget shows current progress. Panel works on desktop and mobile.

#### Add home screen summary widgets
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (part of water tracker delivery)
- **Description:** Implement the 3-widget row on the home screen (above nav bar, below creature): steps ring, water ring, tasks done count. Initially only water is functional; steps and tasks show placeholder state. Tapping a widget opens the corresponding panel or focus screen.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.10: Home screen summary widgets]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Three widgets render below Glim. Water widget reflects live data. Tapping water widget opens the water panel.

---

### PHASE 3: Second companion-mode tracker

#### Build step tracking
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High
- **Description:** Companion mode panel with numpad-style number input (replace-style: latest entry for the day wins). Progress ring fills through tier milestones (2,500 / 5,000 / 7,500 / 10,000). Integrates with `glimReact()` for tier-reached and milestone reactions. Home screen widget updates.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.3: Step tracking], [See Project Docs Section 8.8: Steps schema]
- **Acceptance criteria:** User can type step count, see progress through tiers, and see Glim react to milestones. Replace-style logic works (second entry same day overwrites first). Home screen steps widget is live.

#### Build nutrition quick-add panel
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High
- **Description:** Companion mode panel with: progress rings for protein/fiber/fruit/veg at top, scrollable grid of frequent library items, "raw add" button for direct nutrient entry, "new food" button to add to library. Tapping a library item logs 1 unit immediately with undo toast. Long-press for quantity adjustment. Fruit and veggie are separate in the panel, combined on home screen.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.8: Nutrition and Nutrition Library schemas]
- **Acceptance criteria:** User can quick-add from library, raw-add nutrients, and create new food items. Progress rings update. Data persists.

---

### PHASE 4: First focus-mode feature (to-do list)

#### Build focus mode transition
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High (prerequisite for to-do list)
- **Description:** Build the focus mode UI shell: creature/background fade out over 300ms, full-screen view fades in, mini-Glim (face only, ~40px mobile / ~48px desktop) appears in top-right header corner. Back arrow returns to companion mode. Mini-Glim still shows speech bubbles and reactions.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.10: Focus mode details]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Smooth transition between companion and focus modes. Mini-Glim renders with eyes, pupils, antennae bobbles, blush. Speech bubbles work on mini-Glim. Back navigation works on both mouse and touch.

#### Build to-do list
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High
- **Description:** Focus mode screen. Recurring tasks (Approach 2: definitions + daily completion logs) pinned at top. One-off tasks below with add, complete, delete, reorder. Completed tasks move to collapsible "done (N)" section after 500ms checkmark animation. Tapping completed task un-completes it. No subtasks. No daily goals integration. Integrates with `glimReact()`. Home screen tasks widget shows total completed today.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.4: To-do lists], [See Project Docs Section 8.8: Todos schema]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Can add one-off and recurring tasks. Recurring tasks reappear unchecked each day. Completed tasks in done section. Reorder works. Glim reacts. Home screen widget is live.

---

### PHASE 5: Remaining features (any order, based on priority)

#### Build pomodoro / time tracking
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** High
- **Description:** Focus mode screen. Pomodoro timer with configurable session/break durations. Custom work categories (user-defined: academic, housework, etc.). Auto-logs completed sessions. Manual session add for retroactive/partial logging. Session history for today. Per-category time totals.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.4: Pomodoro / time tracking], [See Project Docs Section 8.8: Pomodoro schema]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Timer counts down, logs session on completion. Manual add works. Categories tracked. Glim reacts to completed sessions and breaks.

#### Build workout / exercise logging
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** Medium
- **Description:** Focus mode screen. Predefined categories (cardio, strength, flexibility, sports, other) with custom types. Duration + intensity (light/moderate/vigorous) + notes. Per-category/intensity weekly minute goals. Resets move reminder timer on log.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 8.3: Exercise / workout tracking], [See Project Docs Section 8.8: Exercise schema]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Can log workout with category, type, duration, intensity. Custom types persist. Goals tracked per category. Glim reacts with category-aware commentary.

#### Build nutrition library management (focus mode)
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** Medium
- **Description:** Focus mode screen for managing the personal food library. Add new items (name, protein, fiber, fruit/veggie servings, unit label). Edit existing items. Hide/unhide items. Accessible from nutrition quick-add panel via "new food" button or from settings.
- **Acceptance criteria:** Can create, edit, and hide food items. Changes reflected in quick-add panel immediately.

#### Build dashboard (focus mode)
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (design)
- **Priority:** Medium
- **Description:** Focus mode screen showing all tracker progress in one view. Progress rings/bars for each tracker, today's totals, streaks, recent activity. The comprehensive view that complements the 3-widget home screen summary.
- **Acceptance criteria:** All active trackers visible with current progress. Tapping a tracker navigates to its panel/screen.

#### Sync service implementation
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (architecture) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** Medium (deferred until multiple trackers exist)
- **Description:** Build the background sync service that pushes local events to Firestore and pulls remote events. Approach 2 architecture: localStorage remains primary read/write layer, sync service runs on a timer and on `visibilitychange` events. Design the module so future trackers plug in by registering their domain and sync strategy.
- **Context/References:** [See Handoff Session Log 2026-03-26: Approach 2 confirmed], [See Project Docs Section 8.11]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Events created on one device appear on the other after sync. Offline writes queue and push when connectivity returns. No data loss during normal usage.

#### Add service worker for offline support
- **Assigned to:** Claude Code
- **Priority:** Medium
- **Description:** Install `vite-plugin-pwa` and configure a service worker for offline caching. Also prerequisite for Web Push notifications.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Section 6.2: Netlify PWA]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Glim loads without internet connection after first visit. PWA passes Lighthouse installability audit.

#### Implement Web Push notifications
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai (architecture) + Claude Code (implementation)
- **Priority:** Medium (after service worker)
- **Description:** Add push notification support using the Web Push API + Firebase Cloud Function backend.
- **Context/References:** [See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-26: Enhanced PWA over native app]
- **Acceptance criteria:** Notifications fire for at least one use case. Works on Android Chrome. Best-effort on iOS.

#### Expand Firestore schemas as trackers are built
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai
- **Priority:** Ongoing
- **Description:** As each new tracker is built, design its Firestore subcollection schema following Pattern A. Register with sync service.
- **Acceptance criteria:** Each new tracker has a documented Firestore path and document schema before implementation.

---

### BACKLOG (low priority, unphased)

#### Implement off-screen adventure behavior
- **Assigned to:** Claude.ai
- **Priority:** Low
- **Description:** Add 2% chance autonomous behavior where Glim drifts off-screen. Requires `isOffScreenRef`, `OFFSCREEN_RETURN` message array (20 messages), and pointer activity listeners.
- **Acceptance criteria:** Glim drifts off-screen, returns on pointer activity after grace period.

## 3.2 Changes for Testing

> Items that need browser verification. Include file paths, what to look for.

### 2026-03-25 iPhone installation
- **How to test:** Open Netlify URL in Safari on iPhone → Share → Add to Home Screen
- **What to verify:**
  - Glim icon appears on home screen (purple/teal creature, not a blank page screenshot)
  - App opens full screen with no browser chrome
  - Glim renders and all interactions work on touch (note: touch events not yet implemented - tap/long-press via mouse fallback may be limited)

### 2026-03-25 audit fixes
- **File:** `glim.html` (from this session's outputs)
- **What to verify:**
  - Settings sliders: change intervals, refresh page, confirm values persist
  - Purr then wait: confirm only one blink cycle runs (no accelerating blink rate after multiple purrs)
  - General behavior: watch for 5-10 minutes, confirm behaviors fire at roughly expected rates
  - Bug chase: over several chases, catches should outnumber misses (~60/40)
  - All existing interactions still work: click, double-click, long press, drag, shake, rapid click

## 3.3 Completed Tasks

> **Entry format:** [Task name] - [Date completed] - [Output location]

- File separation (DesktopPet.jsx -> 9 files) - 2026-03-27 - `client/src/components/` (7 files), `client/src/glim-animations.css`; live on GitHub Pages
- Zustand state migration - 2026-03-27 - `client/src/stores/` (6 stores + index), `client/src/utils.js`; live on GitHub Pages
- Hosting migration to GitHub Pages - 2026-03-27 - `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`, `client/vite.config.js` (base path), `client/public/manifest.json` (updated paths), `client/src/SignIn.jsx` (BASE_URL fix); live at https://reitheheroine.github.io/Glim/
- Splash screen implementation - 2026-03-27 - `client/src/SplashScreen.jsx`, `client/src/App.jsx` updated
- PWA icon replacement - 2026-03-27 - `client/public/glim-icon.svg`, `glim-icon-192.png`, `glim-icon-512.png`, `apple-touch-icon.png`
- Sign-out button - 2026-03-27 - `client/src/components/SettingsPanel.jsx`
- iPhone safe area and UI fixes - 2026-03-27 - `client/src/components/Background.jsx`, `client/src/components/SettingsPanel.jsx`, `client/src/components/JournalPanel.jsx`
- Firebase setup and Google auth flow - 2026-03-26 - `client/src/firebase.js`, `client/src/SignIn.jsx`, `client/src/App.jsx` (auth gate), `firestore.rules`, `client/.env.local`, `client/.env.example`; live on Netlify
- Firebase project setup (Console steps) - 2026-03-26 - Firestore database created, Google sign-in enabled, security rules deployed
- Firestore schema design (current domains) - 2026-03-26 - Schema for journal, pokes, settings finalized. Pattern A (subcollections per domain). Auth model and pre-auth behavior decided. Documented in `glim_handoff.Rmd` (Session Log, Resolved Questions, Task Queue)
- PWA setup and iPhone installation - 2026-03-25 - `client/public/manifest.json`, `glim-icon-192.png`, `glim-icon-512.png`, `apple-touch-icon.png`, `glim-icon.svg`, `_redirects`; `client/index.html` updated
- Netlify hosting - 2026-03-25 - auto-deploys from `main`, live URL in Netlify dashboard
- GitHub repository - 2026-03-25 - `git@github.com:ReitheHeroine/Glim.git`
- Vite migration verified in production - 2026-03-25 - live on Netlify, all interactions confirmed working
- Vite migration and project scaffolding - 2026-03-25 - `client/src/DesktopPet.jsx`, `client/src/messages.js`, `client/src/storage.js`, `client/src/App.jsx`, `client/src/index.css`, `client/index.html`, `.gitignore`
- Codebase audit and bug fixes - 2026-03-25 - `glim.html`, `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/.docx
- Architecture review and feature planning - 2026-03-25 - `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/.docx (Section 8 rewrite), `glim_handoff.Rmd`/.docx
- Tech stack evaluation - 2026-03-25 - `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/.docx (Sections 2.1, 8.2, 11 updated)
- Data model specification - 2026-03-25 - `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/.docx (Section 8.8)
- Reaction system design - 2026-03-25 - `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/.docx (Section 8.9)
- UI navigation system design - 2026-03-25 - `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`/.docx (Section 8.10), mockup HTML files
- Project Documentation creation - 2026-03-24 - `glim_project_documentation.Rmd`

---

# 4. Open Questions and Issues

## 4.1 Open Questions

- **2026-03-25** - At what point should the codebase split into separate desktop/mobile entry points vs. continuing with `isMobile` feature flags? [See Project Docs Section 3.4] Decision deferred until 2-3 mobile-specific features are implemented.
- **2026-03-25** - Should Tailwind CSS be removed before or after touch/responsive work? Removing it first simplifies the dependency story, but it's low-impact busywork. Removing it after means touch/responsive code would use Tailwind classes that later get replaced.
- **2026-03-25** - The `glim-messages.txt` inventory file (referenced in Project Docs Section 2.2) was generated during the 03-23/24 session but was not uploaded for this audit. It may be out of sync with the current message pools. Needs regeneration.
- **2026-03-25** - ~~Nutrition tracking scope: full macro/calorie tracking vs. simple meal logging?~~ **Resolved 2026-03-27.** See Resolved Questions.
- **2026-03-25** - Enhanced journaling scope: what specifically makes it "enhanced"? Tagging? Search? Templates? Longer entries? Image attachment? Needs scoping before build.
- **2026-03-25** - ~~Water tracker UI spec needs finalizing: progress display style (dots vs ring vs bar), logging interaction (just +1 vs custom amounts), and full panel layout.~~ **Resolved 2026-03-27.** See Resolved Questions.
- **2026-03-25** - Should Glim's messages eventually be AI-powered? Discussed hybrid approach: static pools for frequent/fast/offline messages, Claude API for daily summaries, cross-tracker observations, journal-aware commentary. Deferred to after Firebase sync. Cost estimate: ~$0.60/month at 10 AI messages/day.

## 4.2 Resolved Questions

> **Entry format:** **Q:** [Question] **A:** [Answer] ([Date resolved])

**Q:** Should behavior probabilities use cascading draws or weighted random? **A:** Single weighted random draw. Cascading draws caused effective probabilities to drift from documented values. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** Should settings intervals persist across page loads? **A:** Yes, stored in `glim-settings` key alongside journal and pokes. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** How should documentation handle features that were planned but never coded? **A:** Mark with [NOT IMPLEMENTED] tags. Don't remove from docs, but make status unambiguous. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** Should Glim migrate from React to vanilla JavaScript? **A:** No. The planned feature set (10+ data-driven trackers with lists, forms, counters, multiple views) is exactly what React handles well. The creature-side timer tax is manageable with documented ref/cleanup patterns. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** How should cross-device sync work? **A:** Firebase Firestore with offline-first design. Event-based data model (timestamped logs, not running totals) for merge-safe sync. Design data model now, build Firebase integration after trackers exist. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** How should Glim's personality integrate with new tracker features? **A:** Centralized reaction system: `glimReact(eventType, data)`. Trackers emit events, the reaction system handles creature responses. Keeps personality separate from tracker code. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** What build tool and framework should Glim use going forward? **A:** Vite for build, React (npm-managed) for framework, Zustand for state management, Tailwind (build version) for CSS. Svelte evaluated and rejected in favor of React's ecosystem and skill transferability. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** How should data be stored across 15 tracker domains? **A:** One storage key per domain (not monolithic). UUIDs for IDs. Soft delete everywhere. All derived values computed from event logs, never stored. See Project Docs Section 8.8. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** How should the UI handle 15+ features on a phone screen? **A:** Hybrid two-mode system. Companion mode: creature visible, simple trackers as bottom panels. Focus mode: full-screen feature view with mini-Glim in header corner. Customizable 4-slot bottom nav bar. See Project Docs Section 8.10. (2026-03-25)

**Q:** What authentication model should Glim use for sync? **A:** Google sign-in (one-tap, most users have Google accounts). Lowest friction option. Firebase Auth handles session persistence. (2026-03-26)

**Q:** Should Glim work without an account? **A:** No. Require sign-in on first launch. Eliminates the local-to-cloud migration problem entirely. Every event written to Firestore from the start, localStorage is purely an offline cache. (2026-03-26)

**Q:** What Firestore structure should Glim use? **A:** Pattern A (subcollections per domain). Each tracker domain gets its own subcollection under `users/{userId}/`. Scales naturally, avoids 1MB document limit, writes are small and fast. Pattern B (flat documents with arrays) rejected due to size limits and inefficient writes. (2026-03-26)

**Q:** How should pokes be stored in Firestore? **A:** Single counter document at `users/{userId}/pokes/counters` with `total` and `lastPoke` fields. Not an event log. Use Firestore `increment()` when online. Losing a few pokes during offline conflicts is acceptable. (2026-03-26)

**Q:** Should Firestore schemas be designed for all 15 planned trackers upfront? **A:** No. Design only for current domains (journal, pokes, settings). Add schemas incrementally as each tracker is built. Subcollection-per-domain pattern makes additions trivial. (2026-03-26)

**Q:** Should Glim be wrapped in a native app for mobile, or stay as a PWA? **A:** Stay as an enhanced PWA. The only meaningful capability gap is HealthKit/Google Fit (requires $99/year Apple Developer Program). Step tracking ships as manual entry instead. Web Push notifications available on both platforms (iOS requires home-screen install, supported since 16.4). If native API needs arise later, Capacitor (WebView wrapper, ~95% code reuse) is the documented escalation path. React Native and full native rejected due to massive rewrite cost for marginal benefit. See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-26. (2026-03-26)

**Q:** What scope should nutrition tracking have? Full macro/calorie tracking or simple meal logging? **A:** Neither. Hybrid approach: personal food library with quick-add items that populate multiple counters (protein, fiber, fruit servings, veggie servings) simultaneously, plus a "raw add" mode for direct nutrient entry without a food item. No calorie tracking for v1. No starter library. Fruit and veggie tracked separately in panel, combined on home screen. See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-27. (2026-03-27)

**Q:** What UI should the water tracker use? (Dots vs ring vs bar, +1 vs custom amounts.) **A:** Progress ring with tap-to-log (+1 per tap). Companion mode panel. Undo toast after each tap. Progress ring also appears as a home screen summary widget. (2026-03-27)

**Q:** How should step tracking work? (Single entry, additive, or replace-style? Single goal or tiers?) **A:** Replace-style entry (user types current pedometer total, latest for the day wins). Tier milestone goals at 2,500 / 5,000 / 7,500 / 10,000 rather than single target. See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-27. (2026-03-27)

**Q:** How should the to-do list handle recurring tasks? **A:** Approach 2: definitions + daily completion logs (same architecture as habits). Recurring tasks pinned at top of list. Task reappears unchecked each day because no log exists for today. Completed one-off tasks move to collapsible "done" section. See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-27. (2026-03-27)

**Q:** How should time tracking / pomodoro work? **A:** Pomodoro timer as primary mechanism with manual session add for retroactive logging. Custom work categories (academic, housework, etc.) so time is tracked per category. Not integrated with daily goals yet. See Project Docs Decision Register 2026-03-27. (2026-03-27)

**Q:** Should features be built framework-first or feature-driven? **A:** Inside-out (feature-driven). Build minimal scaffolding, then one feature at a time, growing the framework incrementally. 5 phases: (1) prerequisites + nav shell, (2) water tracker, (3) second companion tracker, (4) to-do list, (5) everything else. See Project Docs Section 8.2.1 and Decision Register 2026-03-27. (2026-03-27)

**Q:** What should the nav bar layout be? **A:** Home (fixed, nest icon) + 3 customizable slots + more. Default order: home, focus (pomodoro), tasks, nutrition, more. Home returns to companion mode. (2026-03-27)

**Q:** What summary widgets should appear on the home screen? **A:** Three widgets only (rings-only layout): steps progress ring, water progress ring, tasks completed count (checkmark + "N done", not a fraction). Keeps home screen clean with Glim large. Full detail lives in panels and the dashboard focus screen. (2026-03-27)

---

# 5. File System Map

## 5.1 Directory Structure

**Last updated:** 2026-03-27

**Note:** Vite migration complete. `client/` is the active Vite project. Hosted on GitHub Pages at https://reitheheroine.github.io/Glim/. Netlify project still exists but is no longer active. `glim.html` kept as legacy reference.

**Current structure:**

```
glim/
|
+-- .github/
|   +-- workflows/
|       +-- deploy.yml                 # GitHub Actions: build + deploy to GitHub Pages
+-- .gitignore                         # node_modules/, dist/, .DS_Store, *.log, .env.local
+-- glim.html                          # Standalone HTML (legacy, kept as reference)
+-- glim-messages.txt                  # Message inventory (needs regeneration - may be stale)
+-- package.json                       # Root package.json (stub, not the active one)
+-- tater.html                         # (reference artifact)
+-- icon-splash-handoff.md             # Handoff doc from Claude.ai for icon/splash work (done)
+-- project documentation/
|   +-- glim_handoff.Rmd               # This file
|   +-- glim_project_documentation.Rmd # Project documentation source
|   +-- glim_project_documentation.html
|   +-- figures/
|       +-- glim_system_architecture.svg
|       +-- glim_system_architecture.png
|       +-- glim_data_flow_example.svg
|       +-- glim_data_flow_example.png
|       +-- mockup_companion_mode.html
|       +-- mockup_focus_mode.html
+-- client/                            # Active Vite project
    +-- index.html                     # Vite entry HTML (title: Glim, owl favicon)
    +-- vite.config.js                 # Vite config (React + Tailwind v4, base: '/Glim/')
    +-- package.json                   # npm dependencies (React 19, Vite 8, Tailwind v4, Zustand, Firebase)
    +-- .env.local                     # Firebase secrets (gitignored)
    +-- .env.example                   # Template for Firebase env vars (committed)
    +-- src/
    |   +-- main.jsx                   # Entry point (renders App in StrictMode)
    |   +-- App.jsx                    # Auth gate (SplashScreen / SignIn / DesktopPet)
    |   +-- DesktopPet.jsx             # Main logic component (~430 lines, no sub-components)
    |   +-- SplashScreen.jsx           # Auth-loading splash screen
    |   +-- SignIn.jsx                 # Google sign-in screen
    |   +-- firebase.js                # Firebase app init (exports auth, db, googleProvider)
    |   +-- sync.js                    # Background Firestore sync service
    |   +-- messages.js                # All message pool arrays (10 exports, ~726 lines)
    |   +-- storage.js                 # window.storage polyfill (side-effect import)
    |   +-- utils.js                   # getTimeOfDay(), getColors(), pickRandom()
    |   +-- index.css                  # Tailwind v4 import + base styles
    |   +-- glim-animations.css        # All CSS keyframes (extracted from DesktopPet)
    |   +-- stores/
    |   |   +-- index.js               # Barrel export for all stores
    |   |   +-- useCreatureStore.js    # Mood, hue/sat, all animation + drag state
    |   |   +-- useMessageStore.js     # Speech bubble, reminder state
    |   |   +-- useSettingsStore.js    # Intervals with localStorage persistence
    |   |   +-- useUIStore.js          # Panel visibility, journal view/text/prompt
    |   |   +-- useJournalStore.js     # Journal entries, localStorage, soft-delete
    |   |   +-- usePokesStore.js       # Poke counter with localStorage persistence
    |   +-- components/
    |       +-- Background.jsx         # Sky, stars, nebula, landscape, motes
    |       +-- AmbientBugs.jsx        # 5 floating ambient bugs
    |       +-- SpeechBubble.jsx       # Glim's speech bubble
    |       +-- OwlMoth.jsx            # Creature SVG (pure render, all state via props)
    |       +-- PersistentReminder.jsx # Move/eyes reminder bar
    |       +-- SettingsPanel.jsx      # Gear button + settings panel (reads stores directly)
    |       +-- JournalPanel.jsx       # Journal button + panel (reads stores, onSave prop)
    +-- public/
    |   +-- manifest.json              # PWA manifest (start_url and icons use /Glim/ prefix)
    |   +-- glim-icon.svg              # Multi-hue source icon (SVG)
    |   +-- glim-icon-192.png          # PWA icon
    |   +-- glim-icon-512.png          # PWA icon
    |   +-- apple-touch-icon.png       # iOS home screen icon (180px)
    |   +-- _redirects                 # Netlify routing rule (kept, harmless on GH Pages)
    |   +-- favicon.svg                # Browser tab favicon
    +-- dist/                          # Build output (gitignored)
    +-- node_modules/                  # (gitignored)
```

**Planned additions:**

```
client/
+-- public/
    +-- sw.js                          # Service worker (blocked by vite-plugin-pwa task)
```

## 5.2 Scripts Reference

Run all scripts from the `client/` directory.

| Script | Command | Description | Last Updated |
|---|---|---|---|
| Dev server | `npm run dev` | Starts Vite dev server at localhost:5173 with HMR | 2026-03-25 |
| Production build | `npm run build` | Builds to `client/dist/` | 2026-03-25 |
| Preview build | `npm run preview` | Serves production build locally for testing | 2026-03-25 |
| Lint | `npm run lint` | ESLint check | 2026-03-25 |

## 5.3 File System Update Instructions

When creating, moving, or deleting files:

1. Update the directory tree above
2. Update the "Last updated" date
3. Add notes for new directories or significant files
4. Update Scripts Reference table if new scripts created
5. Check if new files need GitHub inclusion/exclusion

---

# 6. Computational Environment

## 6.1 Platform

**Runtime:** Any modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

**Development:** Local Vite dev server (`npm run dev` at `localhost:5173`). Run from `client/` directory.

**Build:** `npm run build` via Vite. Produces optimized static files in `client/dist/`. Run from `client/` directory.

## 6.2 Dependencies

### Installed (`client/package.json`)

| Dependency | Version | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| vite | 8.0.2 | Build tool and dev server |
| @vitejs/plugin-react | 6.0.1 | JSX transform for Vite |
| react | 19.2.4 | UI framework |
| react-dom | 19.2.4 | React rendering |
| tailwindcss | 4.2.2 | Utility CSS (build-integrated) |
| @tailwindcss/vite | 4.2.2 | Tailwind v4 Vite plugin |

### Planned (not yet installed)

| Dependency | Package | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| zustand | npm | Shared state management (replaces 30+ useState calls) |
| chart.js | npm | Data visualization for tracker trends |
| firebase | npm | Backend sync and auth |
| vite-plugin-pwa | npm | Service worker and PWA manifest generation |

## 6.3 Storage

**Current:** `localStorage` via `window.storage` polyfill

**Planned:** `localStorage` for offline cache + Firebase Firestore for cross-device sync (added later)

**Storage keys (current):** `glim-journal`, `glim-pokes`, `glim-settings`

**Storage structure (planned):** Single unified JSON structure. See Project Docs Section 8.8 for schema.

## 6.4 Repository Configuration

**Status:** Repo initialized and pushed. GitHub Pages source must be set to "GitHub Actions" in repo settings to activate deployment.

**Repo:** `git@github.com:ReitheHeroine/Glim.git` (branch: `main`)
**Hosting:** Netlify - auto-deploys from `main` on every push. Build: `npm run build` in `client/`, publish: `client/dist/`.
**Live URL:** Netlify subdomain (check Netlify dashboard for exact URL)

**`.gitignore` contents (at repo root):**
```
node_modules/
dist/
.DS_Store
*.log
```

**Include in version control (once repo is initialized):**
```
client/src/           # All source files
client/index.html     # Vite entry HTML
client/package.json   # Dependencies
client/vite.config.js # Build config
project documentation/ # Rmd docs
glim.html             # Legacy reference
.gitignore
```

**Note:** No `tailwind.config.js` - Tailwind v4 is configured via the Vite plugin, no config file needed.

---

# 7. Code and Style Preferences

## 7.1 Code Header Format

```
# title: [script_name.ext]
# project: Glim
# author: Reina Hastings
# contact: reinahastings13@gmail.com
# date created: [YYYY-MM-DD]
# last modified: [YYYY-MM-DD]
#
# purpose:
#   [Description of what the script does]
#
# inputs:
#   - [Input files/parameters]
#
# outputs:
#   - [Output files/locations]
#
# usage example:
#   [How to run the script]
#
#   copy/paste: [single-line command]
```

## 7.2 Code Style Conventions

**For build scripts (Bash/Python):**

- Include argument parsing (`argparse` for Python, `getopts`/`getopt` for Bash) for script versatility
- Include usage messages so scripts are easy to use by anyone
- Include a copy/paste-ready command in the header's usage example section
- Comment in a visually organized way:
  - Use section headers to delineate major parts of the script
  - Number steps in multi-step algorithms
  - Explain the *why*, not just the *what*, for non-obvious logic
- Write detailed inline comments for complex operations

**For JSX/React code (Glim codebase):**

- Use section dividers (`// ============ Section Name ============`) between major code blocks
- Keep message arrays at the top of the file, logic below
- Follow the ref sync pattern for any value that needs to be current inside async callbacks:
  ```javascript
  const someRef = useRef(false);
  useEffect(() => { someRef.current = someState; }, [someState]);
  ```
- All recursive `setTimeout` schedulers must use a local timer ref with cleanup:
  ```javascript
  useEffect(() => {
    const timer = { current: null };
    const schedule = () => {
      timer.current = setTimeout(() => { /* ... */ schedule(); }, delay);
    };
    schedule();
    return () => clearTimeout(timer.current);
  }, []);
  ```
- No em dashes anywhere in the codebase (use single hyphens)
- Inline styles preferred over Tailwind classes for new code (Tailwind removal planned)

---

# Archived Sessions

[Old session log entries moved here after their content has been captured in the Project Documentation. Kept for reference but not expected to be read routinely.]

(No archived entries yet.)
